soma.positioning_advice:
Michael should publish or speak on these angles in the next 30 days to maintain momentum with top prospects and close 2027 funding gap:

1. **AI-Enabled Foundational Learning Systems (for Gates, ADQ, Schmidt, Google.org):** Publish a 600-word op-ed or LinkedIn article: 'Why Predictive AI is the missing link in teacher coaching' — position SOMA's AI predictive analytics (built on 10+ years of KiuFunza + Uwezo historical data) as cost-effective ($3.12/student/year), government-integrated alternative to vendor-driven AI coaching platforms. Cite QJE evidence. This signals maturity to tier-1 AI education funders and differentiates from startup pitches.

2. **Government Partnership Model (for Founders Pledge, GPE, Open Philanthropy, Arnold):** Present a 15-min case study at a Tanzania education forum or webinar: 'How LearnImpact scales through government systems: Kibaha pilot + Mtwara RCT + Mwalimu Kinara co-financing model.' Emphasize government co-financing (Kibaha District Council) and Ministry integration. This directly addresses Founders Pledge's government-partnership priority and opens PMO-RALG conversations for GPE entry.

3. **Cost-Effectiveness & LAYS (for Founders Pledge, Open Philanthropy, Gates):** Publish SOMA's LAYS modelling: USD 3.12/student/year vs. USD 12/LAYS modelled = 4:1 cost-effectiveness ratio. Simple one-pager. Share with Lorcan (FP) to reinforce DD answers. This is your strongest differentiator against other foundational learning programmes.

4. **Learning Dashboard Democratisation (for Dovetail, VTF, Google.org):** One short case study (blog or 3-min video): 'What happens when teachers see live student learning data?' Feature a teacher from Pangani or Kilindi using early dashboard prototype. This humanizes the 'live learning progress data dashboard' component and primes Ali (Dovetail Track 2) and Sandra (VTF SOMA) conversations.

**Timing:** Publish angle 1 by Aug 7 (pre-second Founders Pledge DD). Publish angles 2 & 3 by Aug 15 (before VTF counter-signature ~July 30 and GPE cultivation window opens). Angle 4 by Sept 1 (pre-Dovetail Track 2 kick-off). Do not lead with Twaweza history — emphasize LearnImpact's independence and new-org credibility.

Reinforces narrative for Sept HF visit. Avoid any Twaweza reference in public copy (GoT sensitivity per briefing).

timamu.insight_of_week: Tanzania Creator Economy Fund (TZS 2B) launches this month (July 2026) with Sept 30 deadline — Timamu's legal registration in Tanzania is now the critical path blocker. Without registration, the Ministry of ICT application cannot proceed. This is a $200K opportunity with 45% probability that becomes 0% if paperwork lags. Prioritize company registration completion by Aug 15 to allow 6 weeks for EOI preparation.

hot_list.why_now: Sept 1 deadline (39 days) is nearest hard deadline. Adobe's $50K grants are explicitly designed for disability + creator economy fit. Rolling applications mean early submission increases probability. This could be Timamu's first anchor funder and builds momentum for larger asks.
hot_list.recommended_action: Complete concept note (max 3 pages) by Aug 20. Frame Timamu as bridge between PWDs + Adobe Creative Suite access + creator monetization pathway in Tanzania. Request call with Adobe Foundation disability portfolio manager this week.

hot_list.why_now: Rolling deadline (Oct 1), highest alignment (93%), lowest effort (2/5). Microsoft AI for Accessibility is explicitly designed for this use case. LOI stage means minimal upfront burden. Must submit once Timamu legal registration confirmed (likely Aug).
hot_list.recommended_action: Prepare LOI template now (do not wait for registration). Draft focus: AI assistive tech stack for Swahili-language creator tools. Target submission: Aug 25 (2 weeks post-registration buffer).

hot_list.why_now: Fund activates THIS MONTH. Sept 30 deadline is 69 days away. Alignment (82%) is strong because Vision 2050 creator economy fund + disability inclusion is emergent priority. Government funding is non-competitive vs. traditional foundations — IF Timamu is registered.

hot_list.why_now: Tier 1, 88% alignment, 42% probability. Google AI for Accessibility commitments are expanding 2025-2026. No hard deadline identified yet, but this funder is actively deploying in Africa. Early relationship-building now positions Timamu for 2027 calls.

Timamu's Phase 2 (gig economy + AI for PWDs) aligns directly with Ford's economic inclusion + technology + disability rights convergence.
hot_list.recommended_action: Research Ford's 2026 disability grantmaking RFPs (likely published Q3). Prepare $500K concept proposal (Phase 2 focus) for Nov 2026 submission. Engage with Ford's Africa grantmaking team via LearnImpact network if available.

hot_list.why_now: CBM's East Africa disability-inclusive development work + assistive technology focus is consistent with Timamu. 87% alignment, 48% probability. No deadline yet identified, but this is relationship-building phase. CBM has deep Tanzania networks.
hot_list.recommended_action: Request coffee chat with CBM Tanzania director. Share Phase 1 learnings (assistive tech pilots). Explore co-funding opportunity for 2027. CBM moves slower but commits larger amounts to proven partners.

hot_list.why_now: Humanity & Inclusion's disability inclusion + economic empowerment focus matches Timamu. 84% alignment, 45% probability. East Africa presence is active. No deadline, but early outreach signals seriousness.
hot_list.recommended_action: Submit soft inquiry to H&I Tanzania office requesting brief informational meeting. Share one-pager on Timamu model + Phase 1 results. Gauge interest for 2027 partnership discussion.

new_rfps.title: Tanzania Creator Economy Fund — Disability-Inclusive Programme Partnership
new_rfps.why_it_fits: TZS 2B Vision 2050 fund launches July 2026. Timamu can position as disability inclusion layer + programme management capacity. Government funds infrastructure; Timamu provides PWD-specific digital creation + monetization pathway. First-mover advantage if registered by mid-August.

priority_shifts.what_changed: April 2025: Major $15M new disability rights commitment (first in Ford's 90-year history) + structural mandate for 25% disability inclusion across ALL grantmaking programs. This is not a new grant line — this is mainstreaming across Ford's entire portfolio.
priority_shifts.action: Timamu must map to Ford's disability rights + economic inclusion + technology + structural change framework. Prepare Phase 2 proposal (gig economy + AI tools for PWDs) positioned as systems-change work, not service delivery. Target: $250-500K for 2027 submission.

priority_shifts.what_changed: July 2026: TZS 2B fund activates. This is a 2-month-old opportunity that was on the horizon in April and is NOW LIVE. Shift from prospective to active RFP phase.
priority_shifts.action: URGENT: Confirm Timamu's Tanzania legal registration status immediately. If not complete, escalate to Michael + legal team. EOI draft must be in circulation by Aug 15 to allow 6-week review cycle before Sept 30 deadline.

out_of_industry.why_it_fits: Young Africa Works expanding digital livelihoods focus in 2025-2026. Mastercard's creator economy + youth economic empowerment mandate overlaps with Timamu Phase 1 (digital creation skills for PWDs). 80% alignment but only 22% probability — suggests MCF may require larger, more established implementer track record.
out_of_industry.action: Do not prioritize in next 90 days. Monitor for 2027 Young Africa Works expansion announcements. If Timamu secures Adobe + Microsoft anchor grants by Q1 2027, revisit with case studies demonstrating scale + retention metrics.

out_of_industry.why_it_fits: Omidyar's gig economy platforms + digital livelihoods focus is directly relevant to Timamu Phase 2. However, 28% probability + 4/5 effort score suggests Omidyar expects larger grant requests ($500K+) and lengthy due diligence. Timamu too early-stage.
out_of_industry.action: Defer to 2027. If Timamu demonstrates $500K+ in committed funding + 1000+ PWD users on Phase 1 platform by Q2 2027, approach Omidyar with Phase 2 scaling proposal ($1-2M). Omidyar moves slower but invests bigger in proven models.

timamu.positioning_advice:
Michael should publish / speak on two themes in next 30 days to stay warm with top prospects:

1. **'Why PWDs Are Africa's Untapped Creator Economy'** — Op-ed or LinkedIn essay. Frame: 9-10M Tanzanians with disabilities are digital-native, underemployed, and shut out of creator platforms due to accessibility gaps. Timamu = bridge. Target: Ford Foundation, Google.org, Mastercard Foundation thought leaders read this.

2. **'Assistive Tech is Infrastructure, Not Charity'** — Speaking slot (webinar, conference, podcast). Position AI assistive tools (speech-to-text, eye-tracking, voice-command) as *productivity layer* that unlocks economic value, not accommodation add-on. This language resonates with Microsoft + Adobe + tech-first funders. Avoid disability-services framing; use creator-economy framing.

3. **Mention Tanzania Creator Economy Fund alignment** — If Timamu is invited to speak at Tanzania ICT forum, Vision 2050 convenings, or government tech panels, position Timamu as disability-inclusive partner for TZS 2B fund rollout. Build visibility with Ministry of ICT decision-makers.

Target outlets: TechCrunch Africa, Devex, Stanford Social Innovation Review (Africa edition), Disrupt Africa. Emphasize Michael's founder story (separate from LearnImpact) + Timamu's distinct model.

cross_programme_conflicts.note: Same funder appears in both LearnImpact and Timamu pools. Coordinate before approaching — never allow simultaneous asks to same contact.
cross_programme_conflicts.recommended_sequence: Decide priority entity first. LearnImpact asks first if active programme ongoing.

monday_brief_summary:
Week 30 (2026-07-23).

SOMA insight: Founders Pledge $5M recommendation advanced to philanthropy partner (July 14). Second limited DD round expected by July 31. SOMA programme redesign (July 2026: assessments + ability grouping + dashboard + Mwalimu Kinara + AI analytics — no TaRL, no WhatsApp bot) must be briefed to Lorcan if DD questions surface programme architecture. Decision timeline now Q1-Q2 2027. Probability 55% → prepare for both acceptance and conditional approval scenarios.

KiuFunza insight: Hempel Foundation Board Tanzania visit Sept 27–30 is the single most critical cultivation event for KiuFunza anchor renewal. Rosa Robinson's unprompted mention of renewal at Oxford (April 2026) + Anders Holm's first Steering Committee appearance (May 27) + formal Hearing Committee process signals HF is seriously reconsidering extension beyond FY2026 USD 587K. Renewal formally LOW probability per records, but board visit + education partners event + school visit with VVOB create unique window. Prepare school experience narrative NOW; do not over-push; let Rosa lead.

Timamu insight: Tanzania Creator Economy Fund (TZS 2B) launches this month (July 2026) with Sept 30 deadline — Timamu's legal registration in Tanzania is now the critical path blocker. Without registration, the Ministry of ICT application cannot proceed. This is a $200K opportunity with 45% probability that becomes 0% if paperwork lags. Prioritize company registration completion by Aug 15 to allow 6 weeks for EOI preparation.

Top actions: SOMA: Prepare 1-page SOMA redesign explainer (assessments + ability grouping + learning dashboard + Mwalimu Kinara + AI analytics). Brief Lorcan proactively if second DD questions ask about programme model changes since June submission. Do not volunteer; respond only if asked. | KiuFunza: Michael + Youdi: Prepare 3-day board visit narrative. Work with Kibaha/Pangani schools to stage compelling teacher-data story (incentives + learning outcomes). Share school itinerary with Rosa by Aug 20. Frame visit as celebration of LearnImpact independence under HF stewardship, NOT as renewal pitch. | Timamu: Complete concept note (max 3 pages) by Aug 20. Frame Timamu as bridge between PWDs + Adobe Creative Suite access + creator monetization pathway in Tanzania. Request call with Adobe Foundation disability portfolio manager this week.

Next deadline: KiuFunza Phase 5 — Hempel Foundation Renewal (Hearing Committee) — 2026-08-31.
